menu_title: This Meeting Is Going Nowhere -- Ending a Meeting That Stalls
menu_desc: Take control: park it, decide, next steps with an owner and a date
title_sub: Stopping a Meeting That Goes in Circles
ch5_sub: Ten minutes left, three topics open, and nobody has decided anything
ch6_sub: Quick fire, three role-plays, and a room that loves the sound of its own voice

plan_row.tema: This Meeting Is Going Nowhere -- Ending a Meeting That Stalls
plan_row.foco: Polite interruption (Sorry to interrupt) + Let us park that + So the next steps are... (owner + date)
plan_row.atividade: The Close card + role-play with people who keep going off topic
plan_row.homework: Record a 2-min meeting close: decisions, parked items, next steps with owners

heading: Fifty Minutes, Zero
Decisions
warmup.intro: Last class you filled two minutes of silence, and you closed like this:

callback.text: Anyway, we have a full agenda -- shall we start?
callback.speak: Anyway, we have a full agenda. Shall we start?

warmup.box_title: Today you have the opposite problem: nobody stops talking.
warmup.box_text: Fifty minutes gone. Three topics open, two of them not on the agenda, and zero decisions. There are ten minutes left, and you are the one who has to end it.

goal.d: Eight words to take back the room
Three role-plays, and nobody wants to stop

heading: The Language of the
Close
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to stop a meeting that will not end

vocab.def: To finish a meeting, on purpose and on time
vocab.def_pc: to finish a meeting, on purpose and on time
vocab.ex: We have ten minutes -- let us wrap up.

vocab.def: To stop a topic now, and keep it for later
vocab.def_pc: to stop a topic now, and keep it for later
vocab.hint: Stop it now, keep it for later
vocab.ex: Let us park that and come back to it on Thursday.

vocab.def: To return to a topic at an agreed time
vocab.def_pc: to return to a topic at an agreed time
vocab.hint: To return to it at an agreed time
vocab.ex: We will circle back to the budget on Thursday.

vocab.def: Away from the subject of the meeting
vocab.def_pc: away from the subject of the meeting
vocab.ex: We are off topic, and we have ten minutes left.

vocab.def: To continue for too long, with no result
vocab.def_pc: to continue for too long, with no result
vocab.hint: To continue too long, with no result
vocab.ex: The discussion dragged on for forty minutes.

vocab.def: What happens after the meeting: who does what, and when
vocab.def_pc: what happens after the meeting: who does what, and when
vocab.ex: So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.
We are almost out of time -- one decision, please.

vocab.def: Agreed: everybody understands it the same way
vocab.def_pc: agreed: everybody understands it the same way
vocab.hint: Everybody understands it the same way
vocab.ex: Before we close: are we on the same page?

heading: The Meeting You Never Got
Back
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a meeting at Oracle that lasted an hour and decided nothing.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: what was the topic, who took the meeting away, and what should you have said?

reading.ch_sub: A short text about the most expensive hour of the week
heading: The Most Expensive Hour of the
Week
reading.rtitle: The Most Expensive Hour of the Week
paras: Eight people, one hour, no decision. That meeting cost the company more than the problem it was trying to solve -- and everybody in the room knew it by minute twenty.

Meetings do not die of bad topics. They die of politeness. Somebody goes off topic, and nobody stops them, because stopping people feels rude. Then the discussion drags on, the clock runs out, and the group agrees to meet again next week to decide the same thing.

The person who ends the meeting is not the rude one. He is the one doing the work. Three sentences are enough: 'Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.' 'Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.' 'So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday, and I talk to the client on Monday.' Then stop. A meeting that ends five minutes early with three decisions is the best meeting of the week.

choices: Meetings should be longer, so that everybody can speak.
Somebody has to interrupt, park the off-topic discussion, and close with next steps.
It is rude to stop a colleague who is speaking.

tf: Meetings usually die because the topics are bad.
They die of politeness: nobody stops the person who goes off topic.
"Let us park that" keeps the topic for another day.
Park it, and circle back at an agreed time.
A good close names who does what, and by when.
'Marina sends the file by Friday.' Owner and date.

heading: How to Take the Room
Back
bank.sub: Interrupt → park it → the decision → next steps with an owner and a date → close early
bank.label: The five lines of a close
items: Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.
We are off topic, and we still have one decision to make.
Let us park that, and circle back to it on Thursday.
Before we close: are we on the same page about the date?
So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday, and I call the client on Monday.
That is everything. I will give you five minutes back.

grammar.prompt: Which one stops the room? Which one saves the topic? Which one ends the meeting?

discovery.html: "Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time."
discovery.speak: Sorry to interrupt. We are running out of time.

discovery.html: "Let us park that and circle back on Thursday."
discovery.speak: Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.

discovery.html: "To sum up, we have one decision and two open points."
discovery.speak: To sum up, we have one decision and two open points.

discovery.html: "So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday."
discovery.speak: So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.

rows: Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.
Let us park + that / circle back + on + day
Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.
To sum up, ... / So the next steps are: who + verb + by + day
So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.
grammar.note: Every next step needs a name and a date. "We will look into it" is not a next step -- it is a promise that nobody made. "Marina sends the file by Friday" is a next step, because if it does not happen, everybody knows who and when.
mistakes: We are running out of the time.
We are running out of time.
Resuming, the next steps are three.
To sum up, there are three next steps.
grammar.mistake_note: Run out of time não leva artigo. E cuidado com o falso amigo: resume em inglês significa RETOMAR. "Resumir" é to sum up / to summarize.

heading: The
Close
artefact.title: Ten Minutes Left. Take the Room Back.
artefact.reason: Decision made: go live on June 27th, with the standard reports only
rows: Parked: the mobile app discussion -- circle back on Thursday
Next step: Marina sends the migration file by Friday
Next step: Diogo calls the client on Monday, 9 AM
Are we on the same page? Then I give you five minutes back.
artefact.footer: One decision, one parked topic, two next steps with a name and a date. That is a meeting.

heading: Nobody Will Stop Them but
You
listenings.text: Diogo, I sat in your weekly sync yesterday, and I want to be honest with you. Fifty minutes, eight people, and zero decisions. Rafael talked about the mobile app for twenty minutes, and it is not even in this phase. You looked at the clock four times, and you said nothing, because you did not want to be rude. Let me tell you what is rude: taking an hour from eight people and giving them nothing. You are the project manager. Nobody else will stop that room. Interrupt, park the topic, get the decision you came for, and give people their time back. And never, ever close a meeting without saying who does what, and by when.
comp: What happened in the weekly sync?
Fifty minutes, eight people, and zero decisions.
What did Diogo do when the meeting went off topic?
He looked at the clock four times, and said nothing.
Taking an hour from eight people and giving them nothing.

heading: The Close,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Sorry to interrupt, Rafael -- I know the mobile app matters, but we are running out of time and we still have one decision to make. Let us park the app, and circle back to it on Thursday, with the client on the call. Now, the decision we came for: we go live on June 27th, with the standard reports only. Are we all on the same page about that? Good. So the next steps are: Marina sends the migration file by Friday, and I call the client on Monday morning to confirm the date. Rafael, you own the app discussion on Thursday, and you send the one-pager before it. That is everything. We are done in fifty-five minutes, so I am giving you five minutes back.
comp: What does he park, and until when?
The mobile app discussion, until Thursday, with the client on the call.
Go live on June 27th, with the standard reports only.
Rafael -- and he sends a one-pager before it.

heading: Sorry to
Interrupt

lines.html: ...and that is why the mobile app needs a whole new design, which reminds me of the project we did in 2023...
lines.speak: And that is why the mobile app needs a whole new design, which reminds me of the project we did in 2023.

lines.html: Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time, and we still have one decision to make.
lines.speak: Sorry to interrupt. We are running out of time, and we still have one decision to make.

lines.html: This is important, Diogo. The app is the future of the account.
lines.speak: This is important, Diogo. The app is the future of the account.

lines.html: It is important, and it is off topic today. Let us park it and circle back on Thursday, with the client on the call.
lines.speak: It is important, and it is off topic today. Let us park it and circle back on Thursday, with the client on the call.

lines.html: Fine. But I want it on the agenda properly.
lines.speak: Fine. But I want it on the agenda properly.

lines.html: You own it: Thursday, and you send a one-pager before. Now -- the go-live. June 27th, standard reports only. Are we on the same page?
lines.speak: You own it: Thursday, and you send a one-pager before. Now, the go-live. June 27th, standard reports only. Are we on the same page?

lines.html: The client will want the custom reports.
lines.speak: The client will want the custom reports.

lines.html: That is decided: out of this phase. So the next steps are -- Marina sends the file by Friday, I call the client on Monday.
lines.speak: That is decided: out of this phase. So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday, I call the client on Monday.

lines.html: Agreed. And we are finishing early, for once.
lines.speak: Agreed. And we are finishing early, for once.

comp: What is Sophia talking about when Diogo interrupts?
The mobile app, and a project from 2023 -- both off topic.
What does Diogo do with the app discussion?
He parks it, gives it to Sophia to own, and moves it to Thursday.
What are the two next steps?
Marina sends the file by Friday, and Diogo calls the client on Monday.

quickfire.situation: Someone has been off topic for six minutes. Stop them.
tips: 'Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.'
Name the reason, not the person's fault.

quickfire.situation: Park the topic -- without killing it.
tips: 'Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.'
Give the topic a day, or people fight for it now.

quickfire.situation: The room is going in circles on a decision. Force it.
tips: Give two options and ask for a choice.
'We need a decision in the next two minutes.'

quickfire.situation: Check that everybody agrees, in one sentence.
tips: 'Are we on the same page about the date?'
Silence is not agreement -- ask for a yes.

quickfire.situation: Close with next steps. Two of them, with names.

tips: 'That is everything -- I will give you five minutes back.'
People remember the person who ends early.

items: Are you saying my topic is not important?
We cannot decide today -- can we meet again next week?
Why are you always the one who ends the meeting?
pressure.followup: Interrupt with the reason, park with a date, close with a name and a day. Never leave a meeting without one decision.

heading: Take the Room
Back
guided.prompt: Ten minutes left. One decision. Close the meeting.
guided.sub: Use the Close card. Five moves, in this order:
chips: we are running out of time
are we on the same page?

heading: They Keep
Talking
semi.quote: This is important. It is the future of the account.
semi.sub: They fight you twice: first they say the off-topic subject is more important, then they ask to meet again next week instead of deciding. Do not apologize twice, do not accept a new meeting. Park it with a date, force the decision, and close with owners.
chips: it is important, and it is off topic today

heading: Your Real
Meeting
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. One decision.
free.sub: Take a real meeting at Oracle that always drags on. Close it out loud: interrupt, park the off-topic item with a date, force the decision, confirm everybody is on the same page, and give the next steps with names and days.

survival.en: Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.
survival.pt: Desculpe interromper, estamos ficando sem tempo.

survival.en: We are off topic, and we still have one decision to make.
survival.pt: Saímos do assunto, e ainda temos uma decisão a tomar.

survival.en: Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.
survival.pt: Vamos deixar isso para depois e retomar na quinta.

survival.en: Are we on the same page about the date?
So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.
survival.pt: Então os próximos passos são: a Marina manda o arquivo até sexta.

checks: I can interrupt politely, with a reason.
I can park a topic and give it a day.
I force the decision instead of scheduling another meeting.
I close with next steps: a name and a date, every time.
I end the meeting on time -- or early.

closing.line: Fifty minutes of noise, and you still walked out with a decision, two owners and five minutes back.
closing.next_desc: Escalating: when to involve the boss, and how to do it without making an enemy

order_audio.text: Sophia takes the meeting off topic, talking about the mobile app. Diogo interrupts politely and says they are running out of time. He parks the app discussion and moves it to Thursday. Then he forces the decision: go live on June 27th, standard reports only. Finally, he gives the next steps, with names and dates, and ends the meeting early.
items: Diogo interrupts politely: they are running out of time.
He forces the decision: go live on June 27th.
Sophia takes the meeting off topic, talking about the mobile app.
He gives the next steps, with names and dates, and ends early.
He parks the app discussion and moves it to Thursday.

preclass.desc: Stop a meeting that goes in circles: interrupt, park the topic, force the decision, close with owners and dates.
preclass.context: Fifty minutes gone, and the discussion has dragged on about the mobile app -- which is completely off topic. Nobody stops it, because stopping people feels rude. Diogo does it anyway: "Sorry to interrupt -- we are out of time, and we still have one decision to make." He does not kill the topic; he parks it: "Let us park that and circle back on Thursday, with the client on the call." Then he forces the decision the meeting was called for, and checks that everybody is on the same page. And he never closes without the next steps: "Marina sends the file by Friday, and I call the client on Monday." A name and a date, every time. Then he wraps up five minutes early -- the best meeting of the week.
quiz_ctx: Because they are out of time and the decision has not been made.
Because he does not like the mobile app.
We are running out of time.
We are running out of the time.
We are running out from time.
What makes a real next step?
A name and a date: "Marina sends the file by Friday."
A promise: "We will look into it."
preclass.tip_sub: Retomar o controle da reunião: interromper, adiar o assunto, decidir e fechar com dono e data. / Taking the room back.
tip_rows: Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time.
Let us park that + circle back on + dia
Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.
So the next steps are: quem + verbo + by + dia
So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.
preclass.tip_key: Falso amigo perigoso: resume em inglês = RETOMAR, não "resumir". Para resumir, diga to sum up / to summarize. E "run out of time" não leva artigo: nunca "out of the time".

blanks.hint: Hint: to stop someone who is speaking / interromper

blanks.hint: Hint: stop the topic now, keep it for later / deixar para depois
blanks.phrase: Let us park that and circle back on Thursday.
blanks.post: that and circle back on Thursday."

blanks.hint: Hint: away from the subject / fora do assunto
blanks.phrase: We are off topic, and we have ten minutes left.
blanks.post: , and we have ten minutes left."

blanks.phrase: Are we on the same page about the date?

blanks.hint: Hint: who does what, and when / próximos passos
blanks.phrase: So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday.
blanks.post: are: Marina sends the file by Friday."

blanks.hint: Hint: to finish the meeting on purpose / encerrar
blanks.phrase: We have ten minutes. Let us wrap up.

preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of a close. Record each one. Calm and quick -- the person who ends the meeting is doing everyone a favor.
quiz_sit: Someone has been off topic for six minutes and the clock is running. You:
"Sorry to interrupt -- we are running out of time, and we still have one decision to make."
Send a message to your colleague complaining about them.
The room wants to meet again next week instead of deciding. You:
Give two options and ask for a decision now.
The best way to close a meeting is:
"So the next steps are: Marina sends the file by Friday, and I call the client on Monday."
"Great discussion, everyone. We will look into it."
"Let us keep talking in the chat."
think: Take a real meeting at Oracle that always drags on. Close it out loud, for two minutes: (1) interrupt with the reason, (2) park the off-topic item, with a day, (3) force the decision, with two options, (4) check that everybody is on the same page, (5) give the next steps, with names and dates. Record it.

Now somebody answers: "Are you saying my topic is not important?" Answer out loud. Do not apologize twice, and do not reopen the topic -- park it with a date and an owner, and go back to the decision. Record it.

complementary.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
complementary.desc: Gene Kranz stops the noise in the control room. He says let us work the problem, then decides and gives clear tasks.
complementary.tip: Watch the control room scenes and write the sentences he uses to stop a discussion and move to action.

complementary.title: BEP 81c -- Meeting English: Action Points and Wrapping Up
complementary.desc: A good meeting has a clear end. This lesson shows how to summarize, assign action points, and close.
complementary.tip: Learn the closing phrases and use them to end your next call on time.

complementary.title: How to End Business Meetings: Next Steps and Action Items
complementary.desc: How to finish a meeting with owners, dates and next steps, so nothing is lost.
complementary.tip: Practise saying "So the next steps are..." with three real action items from your project.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'Last class you filled the silence. Today the problem is the opposite: nobody stops talking, and you are the only person who can end it.' Avise: hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 17. Depois a virada: 'Fifty minutes. Zero decisions. Ten minutes left.' Faca a pergunta do box e cale a boca 90 segundos.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, a short text, and then you end three meetings -- and nobody wants to stop.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' Ferramenta, nao aula de vocabulario.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'park': 'If I park a topic, is it dead? (No -- it comes back, with a date.)' CCQ 'circle back': 'Do you circle back with a date, or without? (With.)' CCQ 'off topic': 'Is an off-topic subject unimportant? (Not necessarily -- just not today.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'next steps': 'Is "we will look into it" a next step? (No -- no name, no date.)' CCQ 'on the same page': 'Is silence agreement? (No -- ask for a yes.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre uma reuniao real que durou uma hora e nao decidiu nada. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta.
teacher.ch3: Transicao leitura (1 min): 'Short text. Read it fast -- then tell me who, in your team, always takes the meeting away.' A leitura e trampolim.
teacher.reading: Leitura + gist (5 min): 90 segundos de leitura silenciosa. Depois o gist. E entao a pergunta que vale a aula: 'In Brazil, would interrupting a director be professional -- or rude?' Dois minutos de fala dele. Discorde de proposito.
teacher.tf: True / False (3 min): Ele decide em voz alta ANTES de clicar, e justifica com uma frase do texto. Rapido.
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para a reuniao REAL dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one stops the room? Which one saves the topic? Which one ends the meeting?' Guie: sorry to interrupt / let us park / so the next steps are.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Interromper, adiar, fechar. Exija 2 next steps dele COM nome e data. CCQ: 'Is "we will look into it" a next step? (No.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): 'out of THE time' e o falso amigo 'resume' (= retomar, nao resumir). Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'Ten minutes left. Three topics open. Nobody has decided anything.' Suba a tensao.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia was in your meeting yesterday, and she is not happy.' Toque uma vez, sem texto.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'What does she say is REALLY rude?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read the Close card, then close the meeting out loud, in five moves.' Ele fala; voce marca o que faltou.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a Sophia e voce NAO PARA de falar: mobile app, projeto de 2023, o futuro da conta. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a SOPHIA. Depois: 'Was he rude?' Deixe ele defender a resposta -- e a discussao central da aula.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the close, done well -- 60 seconds.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What did he park? What are the next steps?' -- ele fala.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here you close the meeting, and nobody wants to stop. Three times.'
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e a sala. Ele encerra usando os cinco movimentos do Close card. Se ele fechar sem NOME e DATA nos next steps, mande refazer.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Voce resiste duas vezes: 'this is the future of the account' e depois 'let us meet again next week'. Ele nao pode pedir desculpa duas vezes nem aceitar nova reuniao: adia com data, forca a decisao e fecha com donos.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real meeting of yours. Two minutes. End it with one decision. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas duras, uma a uma, sem aviso. Se ele reabrir o assunto adiado, pare: adiar significa adiar.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these, no meeting of yours ever ends without a decision.' Toque cada uma; tom calmo e rapido.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos encerrando uma reuniao real (decisao + adiado + next steps com nome e data).
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos de encerramento e enviar. Anuncie a Aula 19: 'Next time: the colleague still does not move -- and you have to go above his head, without making an enemy.'
